blocks.content:
Logical operators combine boolean values or comparisons.

| Operator | Meaning | Example |
|----------|---------|----------|
| && | AND (both must be true) | true && true returns true |
| \|\| | OR (at least one must be true) | true \|\| false returns true |
| ! | NOT (flips the value) | !true returns false |

## AND (&&)

Returns true only if **both** sides are true.

blocks.title: AND Operator

blocks.content:
## OR (||)

Returns true if **at least one** side is true.

blocks.title: OR Operator
NOT Operator

questions.question: What does (true && false) return?
questions.explanation: AND requires both sides to be true. Since one is false, the result is false.

questions.question: What does (false || false) return?
questions.explanation: OR requires at least one side to be true. Both are false, so the result is false.

questions.question: What does !false return?
questions.explanation: The ! operator flips the value. !false becomes true.
